Script.name: keydedup_main
Script.script:
-- =========================================================================== -- KeyDedup 3.4 - Mudlet Port -- Original author: Gizmmo (MUSHclient) -- Ported to Mudlet with cecho colors, deleteLine() gagging, enable/disableTrigger -- ===========================================================================
keydedup = keydedup or {} keydedup.scan_requested = false -- guard: only process keyring data when we asked for it keydedup.cap_active = false keydedup.items_byname = {} keydedup.batch_list = {}
-- ========= Helpers ========= local function trim(s) return (s:gsub("^%s+", ""):gsub("%s+$", "")) end local function strip_colors(s) return (s:gsub("@.", "")) end local function strip_ctrl(s) return (s:gsub("[%z\1-\31\127]", "")) end local function squeeze_spaces(s) return (s:gsub("%s+", " ")) end local function norm_name(s) s = s or "" s = strip_ctrl(s) s = strip_colors(s) s = squeeze_spaces(s) s = trim(s) return s:lower() end local function toint(s) local n = tonumber(s) return n and math.floor(n) or nil end
-- Store large IDs as strings to avoid Lua 5.1's %d overflow (max 2^31-1) -- Keyring IDs like 3669611946 exceed this and wrap to negative numbers with %d local function safe_id(s) local trimmed = (s or ""):match("^%s*(.-)%s*$") if trimmed and trimmed ~= "" and tonumber(trimmed) then return trimmed -- keep as string end return nil end
-- Pretty print timer local function fmt_timer(sec) if sec == -1 then return "never" end local s = math.max(0, sec) local d = math.floor(s / 86400); s = s % 86400 local h = math.floor(s / 3600); s = s % 3600 local m = math.floor(s / 60); s = s % 60 return string.format("%dd %02d:%02d:%02d", d, h, m, s) end
-- Aardwolf @-color code to Mudlet cecho color name mapping local AARD_CECHO = { R = "ansiLightRed", r = "ansiRed", G = "ansiLightGreen", g = "ansiGreen", Y = "ansiLightYellow", y = "ansiYellow", B = "ansiLightBlue", b = "ansiBlue", M = "ansiLightMagenta", m = "ansiMagenta", C = "ansiLightCyan", c = "ansiCyan", W = "white", w = "ansi_white", x = "ansi_white", X = "ansi_white", D = "ansi_light_black", }
-- ========= Color Output Helpers =========
local function print_row_colored(aard_name, name_w, timer_str) local output = "" local fg = "ansi_white" local i, n, vis = 1, #aard_name, 0
while i <= n do local ch = aard_name:sub(i, i) if ch == "@" and i < n then local code = aard_name:sub(i + 1, i + 1) fg = AARD_CECHO[code] or fg i = i + 2 else local j = i while j <= n and aard_name:sub(j, j) ~= "@" do j = j + 1 end local seg = aard_name:sub(i, j - 1) if #seg > 0 then output = output .. "<" .. fg .. ">" .. seg vis = vis + #seg end i = j end end
local pad = name_w - vis if pad < 0 then pad = 0 end output = output .. "<ansi_white>" .. string.rep(" ", pad) .. " " .. string.format("%12s", timer_str) cecho(output .. "\n") end
-- ========= Dedup Logic =========
local function parse_line(line) local f1, f2, f3, f4, f5, f6, f7, f8 = line:match("^([^,]*),([^,]*),([^,]*),([^,]*),([^,]*),([^,]*),([^,]*),([^,]*)$") if not f1 or not f3 or not f8 then return nil end local id = safe_id(f1) -- stored as STRING to avoid overflow local name = f3 local timer = toint(f8) local id_num = tonumber(f1) -- numeric version for sorting only if not id or timer == nil then return nil end return { id = id, id_num = id_num, name = name, nname = norm_name(name), timer = timer } end
local function choose_keep_and_deletes(items) if #items <= 1 then return items[1], {} end if items[1].timer == -1 then table.sort(items, function(a, b) return a.id_num > b.id_num end) else table.sort(items, function(a, b) if a.timer == b.timer then return a.id_num > b.id_num end return a.timer > b.timer end) end local keep = items[1] local dels = {} for i = 2, #items do dels[#dels + 1] = items[i] end return keep, dels end
local function show_deletion_report(rows) if #rows == 0 then cecho("\n<steel_blue>[keydedup] No duplicates found.\n") return end
table.sort(rows, function(a, b) if a.nname == b.nname then if a.timer == b.timer then return a.id_num < b.id_num end return a.timer < b.timer end return a.nname < b.nname end)
local name_w = 9 for _, r in ipairs(rows) do local plain = trim(squeeze_spaces(strip_colors(strip_ctrl(r.name or "")))) if #plain > name_w then name_w = #plain end end name_w = math.min(name_w, 70)
local hdr = string.format("%-" .. name_w .. "s %12s", "Key name", "Timer left") local sep = string.rep("-", #hdr)
cecho("\n") cecho("<black:gold>[ KeyDedup - destroyed duplicates ]\n") cecho("<yellow>" .. hdr .. "\n") cecho("<yellow>" .. sep .. "\n")
for _, r in ipairs(rows) do local t = (r.timer == -1) and "never" or fmt_timer(r.timer) print_row_colored(r.name or "", name_w, t) end
cecho("<yellow>" .. sep .. "\n") cecho("<ansi_white>" .. string.format("[keydedup] Removed %d duplicate key(s).", #rows) .. "\n") end
-- ========= Global Functions (called by triggers/aliases) =========
function keydedup_run() keydedup.scan_requested = true send("keyring put all", false) send("keyring data", false) -- send("keyring put all") -- send("keyring data") end
function keydedup_start() if not keydedup.scan_requested then return end -- ignore if we didn't ask for it deleteLine() keydedup.cap_active = true keydedup.items_byname = {} end
function keydedup_line(line) if not keydedup.cap_active then return end deleteLine() local rec = parse_line(line) if not rec then return end local bucket = keydedup.items_byname[rec.nname] if not bucket then bucket = {}; keydedup.items_byname[rec.nname] = bucket end bucket[#bucket + 1] = rec end
function keydedup_stop() if not keydedup.cap_active then return end deleteLine() keydedup.cap_active = false keydedup.scan_requested = false -- clear guard flag
local deletions = {} for _, bucket in pairs(keydedup.items_byname) do if #bucket > 1 then local keep, dels = choose_keep_and_deletes(bucket) for _, it in ipairs(dels) do deletions[#deletions + 1] = it end end end
if #deletions == 0 then show_deletion_report(deletions) return end
-- Sort deletions table.sort(deletions, function(a, b) if a.timer == b.timer then return a.id_num < b.id_num end return a.timer < b.timer end)
-- Save list for the report function keydedup.batch_list = deletions
-- 1. Turn on the Gags (Prompts and Empty Lines) enableTrigger("KD_Gag_Prompt_Standard") enableTrigger("KD_Gag_Prompt_Tag") enableTrigger("KD_Gag_Empty")
-- 2. Send all commands (use string id, not %d which overflows on large IDs) for _, it in ipairs(deletions) do send("keyring get " .. it.id, false) -- send("keyring get " .. it.id) send("unkeep " .. it.id, false) -- send("unkeep " .. it.id) send("destroy " .. it.id .. " inv confirm", false) -- send("destroy " .. it.id .. " inv confirm") end
-- 3. Send a marker to tell us when the server is done processing send("echo KD_BATCH_DONE") end
function keydedup_finalize_batch() deleteLine() -- Turn off the Gags disableTrigger("KD_Gag_Prompt_Standard") disableTrigger("KD_Gag_Prompt_Tag") disableTrigger("KD_Gag_Empty")
-- Show the table show_deletion_report(keydedup.batch_list) keydedup.batch_list = {} end
function keydedup_help() local w = 72 cecho("\n") cecho("<gold>" .. string.rep("=", w) .. "\n") cecho("<ansiLightCyan> KeyDedup v3.4 (Mudlet Port)\n") cecho("<gold>" .. string.rep("=", w) .. "\n") cecho("\n") cecho("<ansi_white> Deduplicates keys on your keyring, keeping the best copy\n") cecho("<ansi_white> (highest timer or newest ID) and destroying the rest.\n") cecho("\n") cecho("<gold> Usage:\n") cecho("<ansiLightCyan> kdedup <ansi_white>- Puts all keys on ring, scans, and removes duplicates\n") cecho("<ansiLightCyan> kdedup help <ansi_white>- Shows this help message\n") cecho("\n") cecho("<gold> How it works:\n") cecho("<ansi_white> 1. Sends 'keyring put all' to consolidate keys\n") cecho("<ansi_white> 2. Reads keyring data to find duplicates\n") cecho("<ansi_white> 3. Keeps the key with the longest timer (or newest ID)\n") cecho("<ansi_white> 4. Destroys all duplicate copies\n") cecho("<ansi_white> 5. Displays a report of removed keys\n") cecho("\n") cecho("<gold>" .. string.rep("=", w) .. "\n") end
-- ========= Install Message ========= cecho("<ansiLightCyan>[<gold>KeyDedup<ansiLightCyan>] <ansi_white>v3.4 loaded. Type '<ansiLightYellow>kdedup<ansi_white>' to deduplicate or '<ansiLightYellow>kdedup help<ansi_white>' for info.\n")
